speaker: YORK
text: Bring forth that sorceress condemned to burn.

speaker: SHEPHERD
text: Ah, Joan, this kills thy father’s heart outright.

speaker: SHEPHERD
text: Have I sought every country far and near,

speaker: SHEPHERD
text: And, now it is my chance to find thee out,

speaker: SHEPHERD
text: Must I behold thy timeless cruel death?

speaker: SHEPHERD
text: Ah, Joan, sweet daughter Joan, I’ll die with thee.

speaker: PUCELLE
text: Decrepit miser, base ignoble wretch!

speaker: PUCELLE
text: I am descended of a gentler blood.

speaker: PUCELLE
text: Thou art no father nor no friend of mine.

speaker: SHEPHERD
text: Out, out!—My lords, an please you, ’tis not so!

speaker: SHEPHERD
text: I did beget her, all the parish knows;

speaker: SHEPHERD
text: Her mother liveth yet, can testify

speaker: SHEPHERD
text: She was the first fruit of my bach’lorship.

speaker: WARWICK
text: Graceless, wilt thou deny thy parentage?

speaker: YORK
text: This argues what her kind of life hath been,

speaker: YORK
text: Wicked and vile; and so her death concludes.

speaker: SHEPHERD
text: Fie, Joan, that thou wilt be so obstacle!

speaker: SHEPHERD
text: God knows thou art a collop of my flesh,

speaker: SHEPHERD
text: And for thy sake have I shed many a tear.

speaker: SHEPHERD
text: Deny me not, I prithee, gentle Joan.

speaker: PUCELLE
text: Peasant, avaunt!—You have suborned this man

speaker: PUCELLE
text: Of purpose to obscure my noble birth.

speaker: SHEPHERD
text: ’Tis true, I gave a noble to the priest

speaker: SHEPHERD
text: The morn that I was wedded to her mother.—

speaker: SHEPHERD
text: Kneel down and take my blessing, good my girl.

speaker: SHEPHERD
text: Wilt thou not stoop? Now cursèd be the time

speaker: SHEPHERD
text: Of thy nativity! I would the milk

speaker: SHEPHERD
text: Thy mother gave thee when thou suck’dst her breast

speaker: SHEPHERD
text: Had been a little ratsbane for thy sake!

speaker: SHEPHERD
text: Or else, when thou didst keep my lambs afield,

speaker: SHEPHERD
text: I wish some ravenous wolf had eaten thee!

speaker: SHEPHERD
text: Dost thou deny thy father, cursèd drab?

speaker: SHEPHERD
text: O burn her, burn her! Hanging is too good.

speaker: YORK
text: Take her away, for she hath lived too long

speaker: YORK
text: To fill the world with vicious qualities.

speaker: PUCELLE
text: First, let me tell you whom you have condemned:

speaker: PUCELLE
text: Not one begotten of a shepherd swain,

speaker: PUCELLE
text: But issued from the progeny of kings,

speaker: PUCELLE
text: Virtuous and holy, chosen from above

speaker: PUCELLE
text: By inspiration of celestial grace

speaker: PUCELLE
text: To work exceeding miracles on earth.

speaker: PUCELLE
text: I never had to do with wicked spirits.

speaker: PUCELLE
text: But you, that are polluted with your lusts,

speaker: PUCELLE
text: Stained with the guiltless blood of innocents,

speaker: PUCELLE
text: Corrupt and tainted with a thousand vices,

speaker: PUCELLE
text: Because you want the grace that others have,

speaker: PUCELLE
text: You judge it straight a thing impossible

speaker: PUCELLE
text: To compass wonders but by help of devils.

speaker: PUCELLE
text: No, misconceivèd! Joan of Arc hath been

speaker: PUCELLE
text: A virgin from her tender infancy,

speaker: PUCELLE
text: Chaste and immaculate in very thought,

speaker: PUCELLE
text: Whose maiden blood, thus rigorously effused,

speaker: PUCELLE
text: Will cry for vengeance at the gates of heaven.

speaker: YORK
text: Ay, ay.—Away with her to execution.

speaker: WARWICK
text: And hark you, sirs: because she is a maid,

speaker: WARWICK
text: Spare for no faggots; let there be enow.

speaker: WARWICK
text: Place barrels of pitch upon the fatal stake

speaker: WARWICK
text: That so her torture may be shortenèd.

speaker: PUCELLE
text: Will nothing turn your unrelenting hearts?

speaker: PUCELLE
text: Then, Joan, discover thine infirmity,

speaker: PUCELLE
text: That warranteth by law to be thy privilege:

speaker: PUCELLE
text: I am with child, you bloody homicides.

speaker: PUCELLE
text: Murder not then the fruit within my womb,

speaker: PUCELLE
text: Although you hale me to a violent death.

speaker: YORK
text: Now heaven forfend, the holy maid with child?

speaker: WARWICK
text: The greatest miracle that e’er you wrought!

speaker: WARWICK
text: Is all your strict preciseness come to this?

speaker: YORK
text: She and the Dauphin have been juggling.

speaker: YORK
text: I did imagine what would be her refuge.

speaker: WARWICK
text: Well, go to, we’ll have no bastards live,

speaker: WARWICK
text: Especially since Charles must father it.

speaker: PUCELLE
text: You are deceived; my child is none of his.

speaker: PUCELLE
text: It was Alanson that enjoyed my love.

speaker: YORK
text: Alanson, that notorious Machiavel?

speaker: YORK
text: It dies an if it had a thousand lives!

speaker: PUCELLE
text: O, give me leave! I have deluded you.

speaker: PUCELLE
text: ’Twas neither Charles nor yet the Duke I named,

speaker: PUCELLE
text: But Reignier, King of Naples, that prevailed.

speaker: WARWICK
text: A married man? That’s most intolerable.

speaker: YORK
text: Why, here’s a girl! I think she knows not well—

speaker: YORK
text: There were so many—whom she may accuse.

speaker: WARWICK
text: It’s sign she hath been liberal and free.

speaker: YORK
text: And yet, forsooth, she is a virgin pure!—

speaker: YORK
text: Strumpet, thy words condemn thy brat and thee.

speaker: YORK
text: Use no entreaty, for it is in vain.

speaker: PUCELLE
text: Then lead me hence, with whom I leave my curse:

speaker: PUCELLE
text: May never glorious sun reflex his beams

speaker: PUCELLE
text: Upon the country where you make abode,

speaker: PUCELLE
text: But darkness and the gloomy shade of death

speaker: PUCELLE
text: Environ you, till mischief and despair

speaker: PUCELLE
text: Drive you to break your necks or hang yourselves.

speaker: YORK
text: Break thou in pieces, and consume to ashes,

speaker: YORK
text: Thou foul accursèd minister of hell!

speaker: WINCHESTER
text: Lord Regent, I do greet your Excellence

speaker: WINCHESTER
text: With letters of commission from the King.

speaker: WINCHESTER
text: For know, my lords, the states of Christendom,

speaker: WINCHESTER
text: Moved with remorse of these outrageous broils,

speaker: WINCHESTER
text: Have earnestly implored a general peace

speaker: WINCHESTER
text: Betwixt our nation and the aspiring French;

speaker: WINCHESTER
text: And here at hand the Dauphin and his train

speaker: WINCHESTER
text: Approacheth to confer about some matter.

speaker: YORK
text: Is all our travail turned to this effect?

speaker: YORK
text: After the slaughter of so many peers,

speaker: YORK
text: So many captains, gentlemen, and soldiers

speaker: YORK
text: That in this quarrel have been overthrown

speaker: YORK
text: And sold their bodies for their country’s benefit,

speaker: YORK
text: Shall we at last conclude effeminate peace?

speaker: YORK
text: Have we not lost most part of all the towns—

speaker: YORK
text: By treason, falsehood, and by treachery—

speaker: YORK
text: Our great progenitors had conquerèd?

speaker: YORK
text: O, Warwick, Warwick, I foresee with grief

speaker: YORK
text: The utter loss of all the realm of France!

speaker: WARWICK
text: Be patient, York; if we conclude a peace

speaker: WARWICK
text: It shall be with such strict and severe covenants

speaker: WARWICK
text: As little shall the Frenchmen gain thereby.

speaker: CHARLES
text: Since, lords of England, it is thus agreed

speaker: CHARLES
text: That peaceful truce shall be proclaimed in France,

speaker: CHARLES
text: We come to be informèd by yourselves

speaker: CHARLES
text: What the conditions of that league must be.

speaker: YORK
text: Speak, Winchester, for boiling choler chokes

speaker: YORK
text: The hollow passage of my poisoned voice

speaker: YORK
text: By sight of these our baleful enemies.

speaker: WINCHESTER
text: Charles and the rest, it is enacted thus:

speaker: WINCHESTER
text: That, in regard King Henry gives consent,

speaker: WINCHESTER
text: Of mere compassion and of lenity,

speaker: WINCHESTER
text: To ease your country of distressful war

speaker: WINCHESTER
text: And suffer you to breathe in fruitful peace,

speaker: WINCHESTER
text: You shall become true liegemen to his crown.

speaker: WINCHESTER
text: And, Charles, upon condition thou wilt swear

speaker: WINCHESTER
text: To pay him tribute and submit thyself,

speaker: WINCHESTER
text: Thou shalt be placed as viceroy under him,

speaker: WINCHESTER
text: And still enjoy thy regal dignity.

speaker: ALANSON
text: Must he be then as shadow of himself—

speaker: ALANSON
text: Adorn his temples with a coronet,

speaker: ALANSON
text: And yet, in substance and authority,

speaker: ALANSON
text: Retain but privilege of a private man?

speaker: ALANSON
text: This proffer is absurd and reasonless.

speaker: CHARLES
text: ’Tis known already that I am possessed

speaker: CHARLES
text: With more than half the Gallian territories,

speaker: CHARLES
text: And therein reverenced for their lawful king.

speaker: CHARLES
text: Shall I, for lucre of the rest unvanquished,

speaker: CHARLES
text: Detract so much from that prerogative

speaker: CHARLES
text: As to be called but viceroy of the whole?

speaker: CHARLES
text: No, lord ambassador, I’ll rather keep

speaker: CHARLES
text: That which I have than, coveting for more,

speaker: CHARLES
text: Be cast from possibility of all.

speaker: YORK
text: Insulting Charles, hast thou by secret means

speaker: YORK
text: Used intercession to obtain a league

speaker: YORK
text: And, now the matter grows to compromise,

speaker: YORK
text: Stand’st thou aloof upon comparison?

speaker: YORK
text: Either accept the title thou usurp’st,

speaker: YORK
text: Of benefit proceeding from our king

speaker: YORK
text: And not of any challenge of desert,

speaker: YORK
text: Or we will plague thee with incessant wars.

speaker: REIGNIER
text: My lord, you do not well in obstinacy

speaker: REIGNIER
text: To cavil in the course of this contract.

speaker: REIGNIER
text: If once it be neglected, ten to one

speaker: REIGNIER
text: We shall not find like opportunity.

speaker: ALANSON
text: To say the truth, it is your policy

speaker: ALANSON
text: To save your subjects from such massacre

speaker: ALANSON
text: And ruthless slaughters as are daily seen

speaker: ALANSON
text: By our proceeding in hostility;

speaker: ALANSON
text: And therefore take this compact of a truce

speaker: ALANSON
text: Although you break it when your pleasure serves.

speaker: WARWICK
text: How say’st thou, Charles? Shall our condition stand?

speaker: CHARLES
text: It shall—only reserved you claim no interest

speaker: CHARLES
text: In any of our towns of garrison.

speaker: YORK
text: Then swear allegiance to his Majesty,

speaker: YORK
text: As thou art knight, never to disobey

speaker: YORK
text: Nor be rebellious to the crown of England,

speaker: YORK
text: Thou nor thy nobles, to the crown of England.

speaker: YORK
text: So, now dismiss your army when you please;

speaker: YORK
text: Hang up your ensigns, let your drums be still,

speaker: YORK
text: For here we entertain a solemn peace.
